study: cowshed substrate experiments (ASIF vs SPARSE, attach floor, live-clone validity, tmutil)
caveats: Medians over 8 samples (Q1), 10 samples (Q2/Q3); primitive evidence, not SLOs.
Run under concurrent multi-agent load; absolute attach numbers run high vs the 100k study's 235 ms. Use relative comparisons.
4 GiB images, 2000 files, 64 MiB IO for Q1. Q1 write column is O_DIRECT+fsync on ASIF but the SPARSE warm-clone path threw EBADF only when its clone had the wrong extension (see finding).

q1_format_comparison_ms_median.verdict: ASIF wins decisively on create and all I/O + metadata (2-5.6x); loses ~75-105 ms on attach. Net: default to ASIF on Tahoe (macOS 26+), SPARSE fallback below it.

ASIF_diskutil.noverify: flag not offered by 'diskutil image attach'

q2_attach_floor_ms_median.verdict: Attach floor is NOT meaningfully reducible by flags: -noverify shaves ~15 ms (noise); -noautofsck no help. The ~235-400 ms attach is inherent to the DiskImages framework + APFS mount + DiskArbitration. If attach must drop, it needs a different attach path (DiskImages2 framework / diskarbitrationd), not flags.

q3_live_clone_validity.setup: 2 GiB image, continuous small-file writer + streaming 128 MiB dd loop inside the mounted volume; 10 clonefiles taken mid-write, alternating sync/no-sync.
q3_live_clone_validity.sync_finding: A clone taken WITHOUT sync can miss the most-recent writes (smoke test: a just-written file was absent from the clone). All clones are crash-consistent and fsck-clean regardless; sync flushes recent writes but is not required for VALIDITY.
q3_live_clone_validity.verdict: Crash-consistent live cloning is reliably fsck-clean and mountable under heavy churn, on both SPARSE and ASIF. `cowshed new` should: sync the mounted volume before clonefile (freshness, not correctness), attach the clone -nomount, fsck (fsck_apfs for SPARSE; diskutil image attach --noMount + fsck_apfs for ASIF) as a cheap safety net, then mount. The delete-and-retry-once fallback is unlikely to ever trigger from write churn alone.

q4_tmutil.observation: Every probe path returned '[Excluded]' — but the test dir is under /private/tmp, which macOS excludes from Time Machine by default. Inconclusive for the real ~/.cowshed state location.
q4_tmutil.verdict: Cannot confirm image-volume TM-default from /private/tmp. The spec's volume-level exclusion should stay as belt-and-suspenders; whether internal image-backed volumes are TM-included by default remains a verification item to run against the real state dir.

critical_finding_asif_attach.summary: hdiutil CANNOT attach ASIF: 'this image format is not supported by hdiutil. Please use diskutil image attach'. ASIF requires `diskutil image attach`.
critical_finding_asif_attach.flags:
diskutil image attach defaults to browsable + noowners; cowshed needs --nobrowse --mountPoint

. Owners: `--mountOptions owners` makes the volume root root-owned → user EACCES on the volume root, so ASIF needs a chown of the volume root after create (or mount noowners). SPARSE via hdiutil -owners on does not have this problem.
critical_finding_asif_attach.spec_impact: 01_storage/02_workspaces attach flows are written with hdiutil flags but create ASIF via diskutil — cowshed-core must branch attach on imageFormat: diskutil for ASIF, hdiutil for SPARSE fallback. -noverify/-noautofsck are hdiutil-only and do not exist for diskutil.
